a: I’m a Full-Stack Software Engineer with over three years of experience building enterprise and customer-facing web applications using C#, ASP.NET Core, Blazor, Azure, and Microsoft SQL Server, with additional frontend expertise in React. I design scalable backend services, build reliable APIs, and develop responsive user interfaces that perform well in real-world production environments. I enjoy solving business problems through clean architecture, thoughtful implementation, and software that remains reliable over time.

I’ve contributed to both high-traffic digital platforms and production-critical enterprise systems. At Digital Gaming Corporations SA, I worked on acquisition platforms for Betway and JackpotCity, building responsive web interfaces, supporting cloud-based services, and contributing to live production environments serving real users at scale. Later, through EXL Services at Ardagh Group, I helped modernize a global production monitoring platform using C#, .NET, Blazor, and SQL-based integrations, delivering stable software used across factories in multiple regions worldwide where performance, accuracy, and long-term reliability were essential.

My journey into software development began in 2018, where I was first introduced to programming through Java, building my foundation in object-oriented thinking and problem-solving. In 2022, I earned the AWS Cloud Practitioner certification, which introduced me to cloud architecture and modern application infrastructure. In 2023, I completed my NQF Level 5 qualification in IT Systems Development at CTU Training Solutions, where I studied DevOps, Java, C#, and solution development. As my career progressed, my interests naturally aligned with the Microsoft ecosystem, leading me to earn Azure Fundamentals certification in 2026 and specialize in building full-stack applications with C#, .NET, Blazor, Azure, and Microsoft SQL Server.

Outside of software development, I’m naturally curious and enjoy learning, building personal projects, and exploring new ideas—many of my evenings are still spent at my computer experimenting, studying, or thinking through future projects. I also enjoy following current affairs, spending quality time with friends, and having meaningful conversations that broaden my perspective. I genuinely enjoy sharing knowledge, encouraging others, and learning from people with different backgrounds, which carries naturally into how I collaborate with teams and approach problem-solving.
